title: mistake scope creep allowed_reads task card
purpose: G5.2 defines mistake scope creep allowed reads task card for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake.
scope: Owns `G5.2` only; siblings under `G5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §9 — Branch G — Verification & quality plane (anti-mistake)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#9-branch-g-verification-quality-plane-anti-mistake).
behavior_steps: When the operator invokes `/continue`, `/autopilot`, `/status`, `/gate`, `/task`, `/verify`, `/lane`, or `/program`, the IDE loads the matching skill from `.cursor/skills/` and runs [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) scripts before the conductor LLM turn.

`/continue` executes exactly one pipeline phase per [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) after preflight; `/autopilot` sets `autopilot.active` and loops until `check-pipeline-blocked` stops pursuit per [A3.2](A3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md).

`/verify` routes to the verifier skill and `verify-router.py`; `/lane` and `/program` invoke orchestration skills—none of these commands clear `gates_pending` or substitute for H2 answers or H3 approval per [A5.2](A5.2-continue-not-approval-self-gate-h1-h3-only.md).

Command stubs under `.cursor/commands/` stay aligned with skill contracts so IDE sessions, [I2.1](I2.1-runtime-sdk-run-local-pipeline-goal-autopilot.md) SDK runners, and headless entry points share the same pursuit semantics.
If a slash command would advance past `hitl.pending` or design gates without explicit operator approval or waiver text in the journal, pursuit fail-closes at H2 rather than improvising.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Flaky test â€” escalation S4 once, then H2 with evidence log; no silent retry loop.
Edge case `G5.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `G5.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Extend verify-router for goal-level suite invocation.
Wire CI: validate-workflow checks goal block when pursuit.mode=goal_autopilot.
Validate `G5.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `G5.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `G5.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: G5.2 defines mistake scope creep allowed reads task card for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake. In practice, the system when the operator invokes `/continue`, `/autopilot`, `/status`, `/gate`, `/task`, `/verify`, `/lane`, or `/program`, the ide loads the matching skill from `.cursor/skills/` and runs [s0](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) scripts before the conductor llm turn, then `/continue` executes exactly one pipeline phase per [a2.2](a2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) after preflight; `/autopilot` sets `autopilot.active` and loops until `check-pipeline-blocked` stops pursuit per [a3.2](a3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md).
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-g 2026-06-28 -->

Scope creep is editing paths outside `allowed_reads` or beyond the task card boundary—often from workers opening full design trees or “helpful” refactors. Controls: Librarian caps ([B2.2](B2.2-librarian-allowed-reads-catalog-composition.md)), spawn contracts, and conductor merge that rejects out-of-scope diffs.

Task cards list explicit file targets; implement-feature workers must not expand. Violations trigger rework, not silent acceptance; repeated creep may escalate H2 with diff evidence.
